bead.title: attempt backend: add opt-in in-tree execution mode
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Projects with very large local data, external services, or benchmark fixtures cannot safely rely on the existing DDx attempt backends for every bead. Niflheim is the concrete case: many ready beads are evidence/validation tasks that mostly run tests or long benchmark harnesses against data and services already present in the checked-out tree. The current backends all move execution away from that tree: `worktree`, `local-clone`, and `docker-clone`. That is correct for code-edit isolation, but it makes data-heavy validation fragile or impossible when the attempt needs untracked local datasets, running service state, or a checkout too expensive to duplicate.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/work.go:83` and `cli/cmd/try.go:96` expose only `worktree`, `local-clone`, and `docker-clone` as attempt backend choices.
- `docs/agent-execute.md:97-104` and `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:776-782` specify the same isolated-backend-only model.
- There is no documented or implemented backend that runs a single claimed bead directly in the project checkout while still preserving DDx's claim, evidence, review, commit, and cleanup contracts.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an explicit opt-in `in-tree` attempt backend for `ddx try` and `ddx work`. It should run the agent in the project checkout itself, not in a linked worktree, local clone, or Docker clone. The backend must be conservative because it trades isolation for access to local data:
- require a clean git index/worktree before claim, except for DDx-owned tracker/worker metadata that the orchestrator intentionally writes;
- take a per-project exclusive worker lock so two in-tree attempts cannot run concurrently in the same checkout;
- record the same attempt evidence and routing/review metadata as isolated backends;
- after success, stage and commit only files actually changed by the attempt plus the associated tracker/evidence changes;
- on failure, unclaim/release the bead and leave an explicit rescue message if the tree is dirty rather than silently discarding edits;
- keep the default backend unchanged (`worktree`) so parallel code-editing remains isolated by default.

NON-SCOPE
- Replacing the default backend.
- Making in-tree attempts safe for concurrent workers in one checkout.
- Solving the existing `docker-clone` remote-daemon bind-mount defect; that is tracked separately by ddx-6967f6e6.
- Changing Fizeau model routing.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/cmd/work.go`
- `cli/cmd/try.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/*attempt*` and adjacent tests that select/setup attempt backends
- `docs/agent-execute.md`
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md`

OUT-OF-SCOPE FILES
- Fizeau routing code
- Niflheim benchmark logic
- Docker runner image contents
bead.acceptance:
1. `ddx try --help` and `ddx work --help` list `in-tree` alongside `worktree`, `local-clone`, and `docker-clone`.
2. Add targeted backend-selection tests proving `--attempt-backend in-tree` selects an in-project execution backend and does not create a linked worktree or local clone.
3. Add a dirty-tree guard test proving `in-tree` refuses to start when non-DDx project files are modified before claim.
4. Add an exclusive-lock test proving a second `in-tree` worker for the same project exits with an actionable lock/worker-in-progress message instead of running concurrently.
5. Add a success-path test proving only files changed by the attempt plus DDx tracker/evidence files are staged/committed.
6. Documentation in `docs/agent-execute.md` and FEAT-010 explains when to use `in-tree`, its clean-tree requirement, single-worker constraint, and failure/rescue behavior.
7. `cd cli && go test ./cmd ./internal/agent/... -run 'Test.*(AttemptBackend|InTree|DirtyTree|WorkerLock)' -count=1` passes.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
